---
title: "Rung 2 · Complexity score"
---

[Setup.]{.lead-in} A candidate graph (known or discovered) and, ideally, the
cross-algorithm disagreement from [rung 1](01-causal-discovery.qmd).

[Move.]{.lead-in} Score how much causal care the problem demands, so you know
whether ordinary attribution is safe or whether you must climb to structural
attribution and validation.

::: {.provisional}
**PSCI v0 is provisional.** The score shown here — a Provisional Structural
Complexity Index — is a transparent placeholder that combines graph size,
density, depth, CPDAG ambiguity, cross-algorithm disagreement, and adjustment
burden. The authors' final score plugs into the same registry seam before
publication.
:::

## What the score reads

Each subscore is in [0, 1] with a one-line rationale; they combine to a 0–100
total and a low / moderate / high band.

| Subscore | Reads |
|---|---|
| size | how many nodes |
| density | how many edges relative to a complete skeleton |
| depth | mean hop-distance from causes to the outcome |
| ambiguity | fraction of edges left undirected in the CPDAG |
| disagreement | 1 − mean pairwise skeleton-F1 across algorithms |
| adjustment burden | mean minimal adjustment-set size |

When you score a single live-discovered graph, the disagreement subscore is
unavailable and the total renormalizes over the rest — the app labels this
degraded mode explicitly.

## Reading the datasets

| Dataset | PSCI | Band |
|---|---|---|
| Toy chain/fork/collider | ~22 | low |
| Layered ladder | ~22 | low |
| ACIC proxy stress test | ~36 | moderate |
| NASA renal-stone | ~31 | low |

A small graph can still be complex: the toy DAG scores near the larger ladder
because it is dense for its size. Complexity is not just node count.

[Caveat.]{.lead-in} A provisional score is a conversation-starter, not a gate.
Read the subscores, not just the total.

Next: [attribute with the structure in hand →](03-causal-shap.qmd)
